title: populus nigra subsp. neapolitana
intro: Populus nigra subsp. neapolitana, also known as the black poplar, is a subspecies of the black poplar tree native to the Mediterranean region. This tree has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating a range of health issues. The extracts from the tree's bark and leaves have been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations, including teas and tinctures, can be made from the bark and leaves to utilize its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
